---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Nils Müller-Scheeßel"
date: "2026-02-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

$$
\textbf{Indices:} \quad
i = 1,\dots,N, \qquad
j = 1,\dots,J, \qquad
k = 1,\dots,K_j.
$$

---

$$
\textbf{Gompertz age prior}
$$

$$
\beta \sim \text{Uniform}(a = 0.02,\; b = 0.1),
$$

$$
\alpha = \exp(-66.77 \times \beta - 2.324914),
$$

$$
t_i \sim \text{Gompertz}(\alpha = \alpha,\; \beta = \beta,\; 
a = 15,\; b = 100).
$$
The Gompertz density is
$$
f(t_i \mid \alpha,\beta)
=
\alpha \exp(\beta t_i)
\exp\!\left(
-\frac{\alpha}{\beta}(e^{\beta t_i}-1)
\right).
$$
---

$$
\textbf{Linear predictor}
$$

$$
\mu_{i,j} = \theta_{0j} + \theta_{1j} \times \ln(t_i),
$$

$$
\theta_{0j} \sim \text{TN}(\mu = 0,\; \sigma = 10,\; 
a = -\infty,\; b = 0),
$$

$$
\theta_{1j} \sim \text{TN}(\mu = 0,\; \sigma = 10,\; 
a = 0,\; b = \infty).
$$

---

$$
\textbf{Latent structure}
$$

\textit{(a) Conditional independence model}
$$
z_{i,j} \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_{i,j},\; \sigma = 1).
$$

\textit{(b) Multivariate probit model}
$$
\boldsymbol{z}_i \sim \mathcal{MVN}(
\boldsymbol{\mu}_i,\; 
\mathbf{R}),
$$

$$
\mathbf{R} \sim \text{LKJ}(\eta = 1).
$$

---

$$
\textbf{Threshold model}
$$

$$
\gamma_{j,1} = 1.5,
$$

$$
\gamma_{j,k} \sim \text{TN}(
\mu = k,\;
\sigma = 10,\;
a = 0,\;
b = \infty),
\quad k = 2,\dots,K_j-1,
$$

with thresholds sorted such that
$$
\gamma_{j,1} < \gamma_{j,2} < \dots < \gamma_{j,K_j-1}.
$$

---

$$
\textbf{Observation model (ordered probit)}
$$

$$
y_{i,j} =
\begin{cases}
1, & \text{if } z_{i,j} \le 0, \\[6pt]
k, & \text{if } \gamma_{j,k-1} < z_{i,j} \le \gamma_{j,k},
\quad k=2,\dots,K_j-1, \\[6pt]
K_j, & \text{if } z_{i,j} > \gamma_{j,K_j-1}.
\end{cases}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
&\text{Observed ordinal traits:} && y_{i,j} \in \{1,\dots,K_j\},\quad i=1,\dots,N,\; j=1,\dots,J \\
&\text{Zero-based transformation for dinterval:} && \tilde{y}_{i,j} = y_{i,j} - 1 \in \{0,\dots,K_j-1\} \\
&\text{Interval likelihood:} && \tilde{y}_{i,j} \sim \mathrm{interval}(z_{i,j}, \boldsymbol{\gamma}_j) \\
&\text{Thresholds:} && \gamma_{j,1} = 0.5,\quad 
\gamma_{j,k} \sim \mathcal{N}(k-0.5,10),\; k=2,\dots,K_j-1,\;\gamma_{j,1} < \dots < \gamma_{j,K_j-1} \\
&\text{Latent multivariate normal:} && \mathbf{z}_i \sim \mathcal{N}_J(\boldsymbol{\mu}_i, \mathbf{R}),\quad \mathbf{R} \sim \mathrm{LKJ}(\eta=1) \\
&\text{Linear predictor:} && \mu_{i,j} = \beta_{0j} + \beta_j \log(t_i) \\
&\text{Regression priors:} && \beta_{0j} \sim \mathcal{N}(0,10)\,T(-\infty,0),\quad
\beta_j \sim \mathcal{N}(0,10)\,T(0,\infty) \\
&\text{Age (Gompertz) prior:} && t_i \sim \mathrm{Gompertz}(\alpha,\beta)\,T(a_{\min},a_{\max}) \\
&\text{Gompertz parameters:} && \beta \sim \mathcal{U}(0.02,0.1),\quad
\alpha = \exp(-66.77 \beta - 2.324914) \\
&\text{Simple independent alternative:} && z_{i,j} \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_{i,j},1)
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\textbf{Threshold model}
$$

$$
\gamma_{j,1} = 0.5,
$$

$$
\gamma_{j,k} \sim \mathrm{TN}\!\left(
\mu = k - 0.5,\;
\sigma = 10,\;
a = 0,\;
b = \infty
\right),
\quad k = 2,\dots,K_j-1,
$$

with ordered thresholds
$$
\gamma_{j,1} < \gamma_{j,2} < \dots < \gamma_{j,K_j-1}.
$$

---

$$
\textbf{Observation model (ordered probit; dinterval parameterization)}
$$

$$
y_{i,j} =
\begin{cases}
1, & \text{if } z_{i,j} \le \gamma_{j,1}, \\[2mm]
k, & \text{if } \gamma_{j,k-1} < z_{i,j} \le \gamma_{j,k},
\quad k=2,\dots,K_j-1, \\[2mm]
K_j, & \text{if } z_{i,j} > \gamma_{j,K_j-1}.
\end{cases}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
&p\Big(
\beta,\{\theta_{0j},\theta_{1j}\}_{j=1}^J,
\{\gamma_{j,k}\},
\mathbf{R},
\{t_i\},
\{\boldsymbol{z}_i\}
\;\Big|\;
\{y_{i,j}\}
\Big)
\\[6pt]
&\propto
\underbrace{
\prod_{i=1}^{N}
\prod_{j=1}^{J}
p\!\left(
y_{i,j}
\mid
z_{i,j},
\{\gamma_{j,k}\}
\right)
}_{\text{ordered probit likelihood}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\prod_{i=1}^{N}
\mathcal{N}_J\!\left(
\boldsymbol{z}_i
\mid
\boldsymbol{\mu}_i,
\mathbf{R}
\right)
}_{\text{latent multivariate normal}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\prod_{i=1}^{N}
\text{Gompertz}\!\left(
t_i
\mid
\alpha = \exp(-66.77\,\beta - 2.324914),
\beta = \beta,
a = 15,
b = 100
\right)
}_{\text{age prior}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\prod_{j=1}^{J}
\text{TN}\!\left(
\theta_{0j}
\mid
\mu = 0,
\sigma = 10,
a = -\infty,
b = 0
\right)
}_{\text{intercept priors}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\prod_{j=1}^{J}
\text{TN}\!\left(
\theta_{1j}
\mid
\mu = 0,
\sigma = 10,
a = 0,
b = \infty
\right)
}_{\text{slope priors}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\prod_{j=1}^{J}
\prod_{k=2}^{K_j-1}
\text{TN}\!\left(
\gamma_{j,k}
\mid
\mu = k,
\sigma = 10,
a = 0,
b = \infty
\right)
}_{\text{threshold priors}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\text{LKJ}\!\left(
\mathbf{R}
\mid
\eta = 1
\right)
}_{\text{correlation prior}}
\\[6pt]
&\quad\times
\underbrace{
\text{Uniform}\!\left(
\beta
\mid
a = 0.02,
b = 0.1
\right)
}_{\text{Gompertz slope prior}}.
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\boldsymbol{\mu}_i =
(\mu_{i,1},\dots,\mu_{i,J})^\top,
\qquad
\mu_{i,j} = \theta_{0j} + \theta_{1j}\ln(t_i).
$$

$$
p(y_{i,j} \mid z_{i,j}, \{\gamma_{j,k}\})
=
\begin{cases}
1 & \text{if thresholds are satisfied,} \\
0 & \text{otherwise.}
\end{cases}
$$
